channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Russia frees detained American as talks with US on Ukraine begin
item.description: Russia has freed an American citizen detained on drug charges, in what the State Department called a "welcome gesture" ahead of a meeting between U.S. and Russian officials in Saudi Arabia. American Kalob Byers Wayne, 28, was released earlier this week to the custody of U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Democrat: Vance used 'same language' as Hitler did to 'justify' Holocaust
item.description: Rep. Seth Moulton (D-Mass.) criticized Vice President Vance for the rhetoric he used during his address to European leaders last week during the Munich Security Conference. “He was talking about 'the enemy within.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Attacks by Sudanese RSF paramilitaries leave hundreds dead in White Nile State
item.description: Sudanese officials and rights groups say attacks by the paramilitary Rapid Support Force have killed hundreds of civilians, including infants, in White Nile state.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas says it will free 6 living hostages and hand over four bodies, accelerating Gaza releases
item.description: A senior Hamas leader says the militant group will release 6 living Israeli hostages on Saturday and the bodies of four others on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: A Chinese navy helicopter has flown within 10 feet of a Philippine patrol plane in a disputed area of the the South China Sea, highlighting Beijing's use of force in asserting its claims to the strategic waterway.
item.dc:creator: Joeal Calupitan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turkey detains 282 suspects in a dayslong operation against Kurdish insurgents
item.description: Turkish police have detained 282 suspects in a large-scale operation targeting the banned Kurdistan Workers’ Party, or PKK.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Border town's residents rebuild in south Lebanon as Hezbollah leader calls for Israeli withdrawal
item.description: KHIAM, Lebanon (AP) — Sabah Abdullah comes to her hometown in Lebanon every morning and sits next to her destroyed home. She is waiting for experts from Hezbollah's construction arm to compensate her for the damage caused by the Israel-Hezbollah war that has left her homeless.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea slams US and Asian rivals for pursuing 'absurd' plans to denuclearize North
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Tuesday criticized the United States and Asian neighbors for pursuing the “absurd” goal of denuclearizing the North and said it will push to expand its nuclear forces under the direction of its authoritarian leader Kim Jong Un.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico said Monday it's awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Now is an 'opportune' time to fix FAA: Former official Billy Nolen
item.description: "We certainly can never rest on our laurels when it comes to keeping the flying public safe," Nolen said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Walz says he's 'in touch with Delta' after crash landing at Toronto airport
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) said Monday he was “in touch with Delta” following the crash landing of a flight at Toronto Pearson International Airport. The flight originated at Minneapolis-St. Paul International Airport (MSP) in Walz's state.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Trump stuns Europe with opening Ukraine-Russia gambit
item.description: European leaders are scrambling to respond to President Trump’s first moves to end Russia’s war in Ukraine, shocked to find themselves on the outside of high-stakes talks about the continent’s security and grappling with a potential retreat of U.S. forces from Europe.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump speaks with Macron ahead of European leaders meeting on Ukraine
item.description: President Trump spoke with French President Emmanuel Macron ahead of a European leaders' meeting about the war in Ukraine, as the Trump administration is pressing forward with plans to negotiate an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Delta jet crashes at Canada's Toronto Pearson Airport; crews responding to plane flipped upside down
item.description: Officials confirmed to Fox News Digital that Delta plane crashed at Toronto Pearson Airport on Monday afternoon. The CRJ-900 jet, operating as Delta 4819, departed from Minneapolis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy travels to UAE as momentum grows for talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: ABU DHABI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with the leader of the United Arab Emirates on Monday as momentum grows for potential peace talks ending Russia's war on the country. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Senators discuss trip to Israel from Tel Aviv
item.description: A bipartisan group of senators led by Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) will hold a press conference Monday from Tel Aviv to discuss their trip to Israel. The remarks are scheduled to begin at 12:30 p.m. EST and follow a number of meetings the group had with Israeli government officials.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy not yet signing US economic agreement 'short-sighted,' White House official says
item.description: A White House national security official said Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "is being short-sighted" in not signing a rare earth minerals deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Woman killed as Israeli forces fire on returnees to southern Lebanon ahead of ceasefire deadline
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A woman was killed and several other people wounded Sunday when Israeli forces opened fire on a group of residents attempting to return to the village of Houla in southern Lebanon, the Lebanese state-run news agency reported.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron found dead: reports
item.description: Kim Sae-ron, who appeared in Netflix's "Bloodhounds," among other projects, was found dead at her home on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austria stabbing attack suspect is Syrian migrant who pledged allegiance to Islamic State, officials say
item.description: Austrian officials said a Syrian migrant carried out an IS-motivated knife attack a day after Vice President JD Vance derided EU leaders on mass migration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Trump urged to confront Iranian regime over repression of Christians
item.description: Iran’s repression of Christians continues to grow, according to advocacy groups and analysts. Earlier this month the regime re-arrested two men who had previously been jailed due to their faith.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Ukrainian spiritual leader says Russian Orthodox Church extension of Kremlin
item.description: A senior Ukrainian Orthodox Church official recently warned that Russian President Vladimir Putin thinks the Kremlin is in a 'holy war' with the West.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Emmanuel Macron calls 'emergency meeting' for European leaders to discuss Trump: report
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has allegedly planned an "emergency meeting" to discuss President Donald Trump on Monday, according to a Polish official.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings, attempted murders
item.description: Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings and attempted murders against Israelis are among the 369 released Saturday by Israel in exchange for hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky bourbon bottles signed by the pope raise thousands for charity
item.description: It sounds like the opening line of a joke: What happens when the pope gets his hands on two bottles of Kentucky bourbon?
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Modi meet at Oval Office as US weighs tariffs
item.description: Before speaking with Trump, Modi met separately with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, a Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he and Putin agreed to begin ‘negotiations’ on ending Ukraine war
item.description: Trump says he and Russian leader Vladimir Putin have agreed to begin "negotiations" on ending the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of hostage deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the Gaza ceasefire will end if Hamas does not release the remaining hostages this upcoming weekend.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Jack the Ripper's identity revealed after DNA match, historian says
item.description: A historian believes he has determined Jack the Ripper's identity through a DNA match of a shawl found at the scene of one of his murders.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
